title: Work
description: Vocabulary related to jobs and the workplace.

entries.definition: A person who works for a company or organisation.
entries.example: Every employee attended the meeting.

entries.definition: A person or company that gives people work.
entries.example: She has worked for the same employer for five years.

entries.definition: Money that someone receives regularly for doing a job.
entries.example: The new job offered a better salary.

entries.definition: The latest time by which something must be completed.
entries.example: I finished the project before the deadline.

entries.definition: An occasion when people come together to discuss work.
entries.example: The weekly meeting starts at nine.

entries.definition: Moving to a higher position at work.
entries.example: She received a promotion after two years.

entries.definition: Someone you work with.
entries.example: My colleagues organised a surprise lunch.

entries.definition: Someone who can be trusted to do their work well.
entries.example: She is one of our most reliable employees.

entries.definition: To leave your job voluntarily.
entries.example: He decided to resign and start his own business.

entries.definition: Knowledge or skill gained from doing a job.
entries.example: Teaching experience is useful for this role.

entries.definition: A formal request for a job or course.
entries.example: I sent my job application yesterday.

entries.definition: A formal meeting where someone asks questions to decide if you are suitable for a job.
entries.example: My interview is on Monday morning.

entries.definition: A written agreement between an employer and an employee.
entries.example: She signed her teaching contract last week.

entries.definition: A period of time when someone works.
entries.example: He works an early shift at the warehouse.

entries.definition: The process of learning the skills needed for a job.
entries.example: New teachers complete training before they start.

entries.definition: A person responsible for leading a team or organisation.
entries.example: The manager explained the timetable.

entries.definition: The place where someone works.
entries.example: Good communication is important in the workplace.

entries.definition: A piece of work that needs to be done.
entries.example: The first task was to prepare the classroom.

entries.definition: Free and able to do something.
entries.example: Are you available for a meeting tomorrow?

entries.definition: Behaving in a skilled, polite and responsible way at work.
entries.example: Her emails were clear and professional.
